guideline_summary: Design for horizontal scalability from day one. Stateless services can be scaled by adding instances behind a load balancer without shared state.
text: Design for horizontal scalability from day one. Stateless services can be scaled by adding instances behind a load balancer without shared state. Avoid sticky sessions; use distributed session stores (Redis, Memcached) if session affinity is required.

guideline_summary: Implement auto-scaling policies based on observable metrics (CPU, memory, RPS). Use predictive scaling for known traffic patterns.
text: Implement auto-scaling policies based on observable metrics (CPU, memory, requests per second). Use predictive auto-scaling for known traffic patterns such as business hours or end-of-month batch jobs. Always define minimum and maximum instance counts.

guideline_summary: Implement circuit breakers for all external service calls to prevent cascading failures.
text: Implement circuit breakers (e.g., Hystrix, Resilience4j, or cloud-native equivalents) for all external service calls to prevent cascading failures. A circuit breaker should transition to OPEN state after a threshold of failures and attempt recovery after a sleep window.

guideline_summary: Use bulkhead pattern to isolate failures. Allocate separate thread pools or connection pools per downstream dependency.
text: Use the bulkhead pattern to isolate failures between subsystems. Allocate separate thread pools, connection pools, or process limits per downstream dependency. If one dependency degrades, others remain unaffected.

guideline_summary: All operations exposed to clients should be idempotent. Use idempotency keys for POST/PATCH operations.
text: All operations exposed to clients should be idempotent. Use idempotency keys for POST and PATCH operations. Store idempotency key results with a TTL matching the client retry window. This prevents duplicate processing on retries.

guideline_summary: Implement the three pillars of observability: structured logging, distributed tracing, and metrics. Use correlation IDs across all services.
text: Implement the three pillars of observability: structured logging (JSON format), distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry), and metrics (Prometheus/Grafana). Propagate correlation IDs (trace IDs) across all service boundaries to enable end-to-end request tracing.

guideline_summary: Separate read and write workloads. Use CQRS or read replicas to prevent read-heavy workloads from impacting write performance.
text: Separate read and write workloads to prevent contention. Use read replicas or CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) with an event store. For heavy read workloads, introduce a caching layer (Redis, Memcached) in front of the read path.

guideline_summary: Define a clear data ownership model. Each microservice owns its data store. No direct cross-service database access.
text: Define a clear data ownership model. In a microservices architecture, each service must own its data store exclusively. Cross-service data access must occur through well-defined APIs or events, never through shared database connections.

guideline_summary: Right-size compute resources. Use spot/preemptible instances for fault-tolerant batch workloads to reduce costs by up to 90%.
text: Right-size compute resources using profiling data and cloud cost advisor recommendations. Use spot or preemptible instances for fault-tolerant batch workloads to reduce costs up to 90%. Implement request-based auto-scaling (e.g., KEDA) to scale to zero during idle periods.

guideline_summary: Design for multi-AZ deployment. Distribute traffic across at least 3 availability zones to tolerate AZ-level failures.
text: Design for multi-Availability Zone (AZ) deployment. Distribute application instances and database replicas across at least 3 AZs. Configure health checks and failover policies so traffic is automatically rerouted on AZ failure. This achieves 99.99% baseline availability.

guideline_summary: Version all APIs from v1. Use URI versioning (/api/v1/) or header versioning. Never break existing clients.
text: Version all APIs starting from v1. Use URI versioning (/api/v1/resource) or header-based versioning (Accept: application/vnd.api+json;version=1). Never make breaking changes to existing API versions. Deprecate old versions with at least 6 months notice.

guideline_summary: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff and jitter for all transient failures. Set maximum retry limits.
text: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff and random jitter for all transient failures (network timeouts, 5xx errors). Set a maximum retry count (typically 3-5 attempts). Use a dead letter queue for messages that exceed the retry limit.
